name: liveworld-video-world-models
description: Enable video world models to simulate persistent 4D dynamics in occluded regions by decoupling world evolution from view-dependent rendering. Maintain virtual monitors tracking entity state even when unobserved, enabling realistic continuation of videos where objects move out of frame and re-enter with physically plausible trajectories.
paperTitle: LiveWorld: Simulating Out-of-Sight Dynamics in Video World Models
content:
## Problem

Current video world models "freeze" unobserved objects at their last-seen state, failing to simulate out-of-frame dynamics. Objects leaving the camera view remain static, causing unrealistic discontinuities when they re-enter. This fundamental limitation prevents coherent long-horizon video understanding and simulation.

## Actionable Technique: Structured World-State Decomposition

Factor global state into static backgrounds and dynamic entities:

```python
# Decoupled world representation
class LiveWorldState:
    def __init__(self):
        # Static background: temporal-invariant 3D geometry
        self.background_3d = None  # Point cloud or SDF

        # Dynamic entities: evolving state (position, orientation, appearance)
        self.entities = {}  # entity_id -> {position, pose, appearance, velocity}

        # Virtual monitors: track unobserved entities
        self.virtual_monitors = {}  # monitor_id -> entity tracking context

    def decompose_video_frame(self, frame, vlm_segmentation):
        """Extract dynamic entities from static background."""
        # VLM segmentation: identify objects and regions
        segmentations = vlm_segmentation(frame)

        # Classify as static or dynamic
        for obj in segmentations:
            if self.is_stationary(obj):
                # Add to background
                self.add_to_background_pointcloud(obj)
            else:
                # Track as dynamic entity
                entity_id = self.create_entity(obj)
                self.entities[entity_id] = {
                    "pose": extract_pose(obj),
                    "appearance": extract_appearance(obj),
                    "velocity": estimate_velocity(obj)
                }

    def is_stationary(self, object_region):
        """Detect if object moves between frames."""
        # Optical flow analysis
        flow = compute_optical_flow(object_region)
        return flow.magnitude() < motion_threshold
```

## Actionable Technique: Monitor-Driven Autonomous Entity Evolution

Deploy virtual monitors at entity locations to track unobserved dynamics:

```python
# Virtual monitor system for out-of-view tracking
class VirtualMonitor:
    def __init__(self, entity_id, initial_state):
        self.entity_id = entity_id
        self.state = initial_state
        self.evolution_history = [initial_state]
        self.observation_count = 0

    def predict_evolution(self, time_delta, physics_model):
        """Autonomously evolve entity state when unobserved."""
        current_state = self.state
        evolved_state = physics_model.integrate(
            current_state,
            time_delta
        )

        self.state = evolved_state
        self.evolution_history.append(evolved_state)

    def receive_observation(self, observation):
        """Update when entity re-enters view."""
        # Fuse observation with evolved state
        # Weight by confidence in both
        confidence_evolved = 0.7  # Trust model prediction
        confidence_obs = 0.9      # Observations most reliable

        self.state = (
            confidence_evolved * self.state +
            confidence_obs * observation
        ) / (confidence_evolved + confidence_obs)

        self.observation_count += 1

def evolve_world_state(world_state, time_delta, physics_model):
    """Evolve all entities, observed and unobserved."""
    for monitor in world_state.virtual_monitors.values():
        monitor.predict_evolution(time_delta, physics_model)

    return world_state
```

## Actionable Technique: Unified State-Conditioned Video Diffusion

Use single backbone for autonomous evolution and state-aware rendering:

```python
# Dual-purpose diffusion backbone
class StateConditionedDiffusion(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        self.video_diffusion_model = VideoTransformer()

    def forward_evolution(self, entity_state, time_delta):
        """Autonomous evolution when unobserved."""
        # Condition on entity state only
        evolved_state = self.video_diffusion_model(
            condition=entity_state,
            task="evolution"  # Track state progression
        )
        return evolved_state

    def forward_rendering(self, world_state, camera_pose, noise_schedule):
        """Render world state from camera viewpoint."""
        # Condition on world state + camera
        rendered_frame = self.video_diffusion_model(
            condition=(world_state, camera_pose),
            task="rendering"
        )
        return rendered_frame

    def forward_full_step(self, world_state, camera_pose, time_delta, physics_model):
        """Combined evolution + rendering."""
        # Step 1: Evolve all entities
        for entity in world_state.entities.values():
            entity["state"] = self.forward_evolution(entity["state"], time_delta)

        # Step 2: Render from camera
        rendered_frame = self.forward_rendering(world_state, camera_pose)

        return rendered_frame
```

## Actionable Technique: LiveBench Evaluation Framework

Quantify out-of-sight dynamics with structured benchmark:

```python
# Evaluation of persistent world dynamics
class LiveBenchEvaluator:
    def __init__(self):
        self.scenes = []  # 100 diverse scenes
        self.trajectories = []  # 400 sequences per scene

    def evaluate_out_of_sight_persistence(self, model, scene, trajectory):
        """Measure model's ability to continue unobserved dynamics."""
        # Trajectory: entity leaves frame, stays occluded, re-enters

        # Phase 1: Observe until entity exits
        visible_frames = trajectory.frames_before_exit
        for frame in visible_frames:
            model.observe(frame)

        # Phase 2: Entity out of frame
        occluded_steps = trajectory.occluded_frame_count
        predictions = []
        for _ in range(occluded_steps):
            pred_frame = model.predict_next()
            predictions.append(pred_frame)

        # Phase 3: Measure consistency on re-entry
        reentry_frames = trajectory.frames_after_reentry
        reentry_errors = []
        for pred, true in zip(predictions[-len(reentry_frames):], reentry_frames):
            # Spatial metrics
            spatial_error = spatial_l2_distance(pred, true)
            # Semantic consistency
            semantic_error = vl m_feature_distance(pred, true)

            reentry_errors.append({
                "spatial": spatial_error,
                "semantic": semantic_error
            })

        return reentry_errors
```

## When NOT to use

- Highly dynamic scenes (fluid, smoke) where deterministic evolution impossible
- Multi-object interactions requiring explicit collision handling
- Real-time applications where per-entity tracking overhead unacceptable

## Benchmark Results

| Metric | Baseline (Freeze) | LiveWorld | Improvement |
|--------|------------------|-----------|------------|
| PSNR (re-entry frames) | 18.2 | 22.6 | +24% |
| SSIM (out-of-view) | 0.62 | 0.74 | +19% |
| Semantic consistency (LPIPS) | 0.45 | 0.28 | -38% |

## Architecture Parameters

| Component | Value | Notes |
|-----------|-------|-------|
| Background representation | Point cloud (Stream3R) | SLAM-based 3D |
| Max virtual monitors | 20-30 | Per scene entity count |
| Monitor update frequency | Every 5 frames | Balance tracking vs. compute |
| Physics model | MLP (learned) | Task-specific dynamics |
| Diffusion steps | 50 | Evolution generation |
| Rendering steps | 25 | Frame synthesis |

## References

Paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.07145
